title: Consonants: Semivowels & Sibilants
description: In this episode, we cover the remaining consonants — the semivowels (य, र, ल, व) and the sibilants/fricatives (श, ष, स, ह). These eight letters complete the Hindi consonant set! Semivowels flow smoothly like vowels, while sibilants create hissing or breathy sounds.
keyPoints: Semivowels: य (ya), र (ra), ल (la), व (va) — smooth, vowel-like consonants
Sibilants: श (sha), ष (ṣha), स (sa) — three distinct 's/sh' sounds
ह (ha) — the aspirate, a soft breathy sound
श is palatal 'sh', ष is retroflex 'sh', and स is dental 's'

hint: Draw three vertical lines of descending height from left to right, connected at the top by the headline. The leftmost line is the tallest.

text: Which letter represents the DENTAL 's' sound (as in 'sun')?

instruction: Match each word to its English meaning

hint: Draw a shape that looks like a person sitting — a rounded body with a small curve at the top. Add the headline.

title: Semivowels & Sibilants — Recap
takeaways: You learned 4 semivowels: य (ya), र (ra), ल (la), व (va)
You learned 3 sibilants: श (sha/palatal), ष (ṣha/retroflex), स (sa/dental)
Hindi has THREE 'sh/s' sounds — श, ष, स — each made at a different mouth position
New words: यात्रा (journey), राजा (king), लड़का (boy), वन (forest), शेर (lion), हाथी (elephant)
Congratulations — you now know ALL the Hindi consonants!
